prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_assessment_entity student_1)
(allocate_priority_slot_to_assessment student_1 priority_slot_1)
(reserve_exam_window_for_assessment student_1 exam_window_1)
(confirm_reserved_exam_window student_1 exam_window_1)
(assign_recovery_option_to_student_choice student_1 student_priority_choice_1 recovery_option_standard)
(process_student_recovery_attempt student_1 student_priority_choice_1 exam_window_1 recovery_option_standard)
(release_exam_window_reservation student_1 exam_window_1)
(register_assessment_entity instructor_1)
(allocate_priority_slot_to_assessment instructor_1 priority_slot_2)
(reserve_exam_window_for_assessment instructor_1 exam_window_1)
(confirm_reserved_exam_window instructor_1 exam_window_1)
(assign_recovery_option_to_instructor_choice instructor_1 instructor_priority_choice_1 recovery_option_standard)
(process_instructor_recovery_attempt instructor_1 instructor_priority_choice_1 exam_window_1 recovery_option_standard)
(release_exam_window_reservation instructor_1 exam_window_1)
(assemble_grade_batch_standard student_1 instructor_1 student_priority_choice_1 instructor_priority_choice_1 grade_batch_1)
(reserve_exam_window_for_assessment student_1 exam_window_1)
(lock_grade_batch_for_weight_binding grade_batch_1 student_1 exam_window_1)
(finalize_student_progress student_1 grade_batch_1)
(finalize_instructor_progress instructor_1 grade_batch_1)
(release_exam_window_reservation student_1 exam_window_1)
(register_assessment_entity assessment_instance_1)
(allocate_priority_slot_to_assessment assessment_instance_1 priority_slot_3)
(reserve_exam_window_for_assessment assessment_instance_1 exam_window_1)
(confirm_reserved_exam_window assessment_instance_1 exam_window_1)
(bind_weight_component_to_instance_and_batch assessment_instance_1 weight_component_a grade_batch_1)
(apply_weight_component_to_instance assessment_instance_1 weight_component_a grade_batch_1 exam_window_1)
(apply_grade_adjustment_to_instance assessment_instance_1 grade_adjustment_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_assessment assessment_instance_1 reviewer_1)
(assign_review_tasks_primary assessment_instance_1 grade_adjustment_1 reviewer_1 weight_component_a grade_batch_1)
(assign_regulation_clause_to_instance assessment_instance_1 regulation_clause_1)
(initiate_instance_verification assessment_instance_1 regulation_clause_1 weight_component_a grade_batch_1)
(finalize_instance_after_verification assessment_instance_1)
(process_resubmission_for_assessment assessment_instance_1 resubmission_token_1 exam_window_1)
(apply_resubmission_and_release_priority_slot_for_instance assessment_instance_1 priority_slot_3 resubmission_token_1)
(release_exam_window_reservation assessment_instance_1 exam_window_1)
(unassign_reviewer_from_assessment assessment_instance_1 reviewer_1)
(revert_grade_adjustment_from_instance assessment_instance_1 grade_adjustment_1)
(remove_regulation_clause_from_instance assessment_instance_1 regulation_clause_1)
(register_assessment_entity assessment_instance_2)
(allocate_priority_slot_to_assessment assessment_instance_2 priority_slot_3)
(reserve_exam_window_for_assessment assessment_instance_2 exam_window_1)
(confirm_reserved_exam_window assessment_instance_2 exam_window_1)
(bind_weight_component_to_instance_and_batch assessment_instance_2 weight_component_b grade_batch_1)
(apply_weight_component_to_instance assessment_instance_2 weight_component_b grade_batch_1 exam_window_1)
(apply_grade_adjustment_to_instance assessment_instance_2 grade_adjustment_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_assessment assessment_instance_2 reviewer_1)
(assign_review_tasks_primary assessment_instance_2 grade_adjustment_1 reviewer_1 weight_component_b grade_batch_1)
(assign_regulation_clause_to_instance assessment_instance_2 regulation_clause_1)
(initiate_instance_verification assessment_instance_2 regulation_clause_1 weight_component_b grade_batch_1)
(finalize_instance_after_verification assessment_instance_2)
(process_resubmission_for_assessment assessment_instance_2 resubmission_token_1 exam_window_1)
(apply_resubmission_and_release_priority_slot_for_instance assessment_instance_2 priority_slot_3 resubmission_token_1)
(release_exam_window_reservation assessment_instance_2 exam_window_1)
(unassign_reviewer_from_assessment assessment_instance_2 reviewer_1)
(revert_grade_adjustment_from_instance assessment_instance_2 grade_adjustment_1)
(remove_regulation_clause_from_instance assessment_instance_2 regulation_clause_1)
(reserve_exam_window_for_assessment student_1 exam_window_1)
(process_resubmission_for_assessment student_1 resubmission_token_1 exam_window_1)
(apply_resubmission_and_release_priority_slot_for_student student_1 priority_slot_1 resubmission_token_1)
(release_exam_window_reservation student_1 exam_window_1)
(reserve_exam_window_for_assessment instructor_1 exam_window_1)

message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for weighted assessment priority management. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves solving a planning problem in PDDL for weighted assessment priority management.  
- The goal is to output an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, formatted as (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).  

## Preparing the Output  
- Each action must be written on a new line in the specified PDDL format.  
- The plan should only include executable actions without additional explanation or metadata.

## Examining Initial Conditions  
- Approval_token_1 is available for assessment_instance_1, but approval_token_2 is not available for assessment_instance_2 initially.  
- Regulation_clause_1 is available, which may be relevant for plan execution.  

## Identifying the Goal  
- The goal is to achieve participant_academic_progress_recorded for student_1, instructor_1, assessment_instance_1, and assessment_instance_2.  
- Participant_academic_progress_recorded is linked to assessment entities, including students, instructors, and assessment instances.  

## Mapping the Path to Goal  
- Achieving participant_academic_progress_recorded requires first getting instance_progression_recorded, then processing resubmission.  
- This involves actions like registering entities, reserving exam windows, and assigning priority slots.  

## Exploring Required Actions  
- Registering entities (student_1, instructor_1, assessment_instance_1, assessment_instance_2) is needed as no assessments are registered initially.  
- Actions like process_resubmission_for_assessment and apply_resubmission_and_release_priority_slot are critical for achieving the goal.

## Analyzing Resource Availability  
- Approval_token_1 is available for assessment_instance_1, but approval_token_2 is not initially available, limiting options for instance_2.  
- Regulation_clause_1 and admin_flags are available, potentially supporting certain actions like granting approval.  

## Exploring Entity Progression  
- Progressing entities like assessment_instance_1 may require completing cycles for student_1 and instructor_1 together, involving ready flags and choices.  
- Actions like grade_batch or recovery paths depend on resources like reserved exam windows and reviewers being available.  

## Sequencing Actions for Progress  
- The process starts with registering entities, then allocating priority slots and reserving exam windows.  
- After confirmation, entities can be marked ready for assessment, followed by assigning reviewers and committing choices for batch readiness.

## Continuing Entity Progression  
- The approval path allows assessment instances to achieve instance_progression_recorded without grade_batch, involving actions like granting approval and starting reviews.  
- For assessment_instance_1, the process includes registering, allocating priority slots, reserving exam windows, and assigning reviewers and regulation clauses.  

- Granting approval requires a ready instance, reserved exam window, and available approval token, leading to the instance being marked as granted.  
- Starting and completing the approval review relies on having an assigned reviewer and regulation clause, culminating in finalized instance progression.  

## Planning Resource Management  
- After progression, resources like priority slots and resubmission tokens are released, enabling reuse for other entities despite initial constraints (e.g., only 3 priority slots for 4 entities).  
- Exam windows and reviewers remain assigned post-progression, requiring actions like unassigning reviewers or releasing exam window reservations for subsequent entity processing.

## Planning Entity Progression  
- Progressing entities like students and instructors may involve assembling grade_batch_1, locking it, and applying weights, potentially sharing resources like exam windows.  
- For assessment_instance_1, the approval path works with available approval_token_1, but assessment_instance_2 lacks approval_token_2, requiring alternative paths like verification, which needs review_tasks and weight application.  

## Optimizing Resource Use  
- Reserving exam windows for students first, like student_1, could allow locking grade_batch_1, then releasing for assessment instances, addressing limited resources like only 3 priority slots for 4 entities.  
- Completing entities sequentially, such as finalizing student_1 progress first, ensures resource availability for subsequent steps, like reserving exam windows for assessment_instance_2.  

## Exploring Progress Paths  
- Finalize_student_progress sets instance_progression_recorded for students, requiring student_ready_flag, ready_for_batch, and a locked grade_batch, but the goal is participant_academic_progress_recorded, achieved via resubmission.  
- Assembling standard grade_batch requires both student and instructor ready_for_batch, suggesting preparing both entities before locking with student’s exam window and finalizing progress.

## Managing Exam Window Reservations  
- Releasing exam window reservations, like for student_1, makes the window available for others, such as instructor_1, enabling sequential processing.  
- Reserving the exam window again for student_1 is possible after release, as long as priority remains assigned, supporting flexible resource reuse.  

## Coordinating Entity Readiness  
- Both student_1 and instructor_1 need to be ready_for_batch and have committed choices before assembling the grade batch, ensuring synchronized progress.  
- Assembling the grade batch requires releasing and re-reserving the exam window, aligning resource availability with entity readiness.  

## Finalizing Progress Steps  
- Locking the grade batch for student_1 uses a reserved exam window, enabling finalize_student_progress to set instance_progression_recorded.  
- Similarly, finalize_instructor_progress for instructor_1 can proceed, marking instance_progression_recorded once the grade batch is locked.  

## Preparing Assessment Instances  
- Registering assessment_instance_1 and allocating the last priority slot allows binding weight components without needing a reserved exam window, simplifying initial steps.  
- Releasing the exam window after student_1 progress ensures availability for assessment instances, addressing limited resource constraints.

## Handling Resubmissions  
- Reserving the exam window for student_1 again is needed for resubmission, possible since priority remains assigned and the window is available.  
- Processing resubmission for student_1 sets participant_academic_progress_recorded, releasing the priority slot and resource token after completion.  

## Managing Exam Window Availability  
- Releasing the exam window after student_1’s resubmission makes it available for instructor_1, ensuring sequential processing.  
- Reserving and processing resubmission for instructor_1 similarly marks academic progress, releasing resources afterward.  

## Sequencing Actions  
- Registering assessment instances after student and instructor progress ensures grade batch locking and resource availability.  
- Applying weight to assessment instances requires a reserved exam window, handled sequentially with available priority slots.

## Processing Assessment Instances  
- Assessment instance 1 is verified and finalized, marking it ready for resubmission processing.  
- Resubmission for assessment instance 1 uses exam window 1, applying and releasing priority slot 3 afterward.  

- Cleanup steps include releasing the exam window, unassigning the reviewer, and reverting grade adjustments for assessment instance 1.  
- Assessment instance 2 follows the same process, registering, reserving exam window 1, and applying weight components.  

## Managing Resubmission Workflow  
- Resubmission for student 1 reserves exam window 1, processes using priority slot 1, and releases resources after completion.  
- Similarly, instructor 1’s resubmission follows, reserving exam window 1 and releasing priority slot 2 after processing.  

- Priority slots remain assigned for student 1 and instructor 1 until explicitly released during the resubmission process.  
- All resubmissions ensure academic progress is recorded, maintaining resource availability for subsequent steps.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
